title: Boil advisory kitchen routine
summary: A kitchen workflow for preventing old tap-water habits during a boil-water notice.
content: A boil-water notice changes ordinary actions: drinking, brushing teeth, rinsing food, making ice, preparing baby items, and washing some utensils. Put a stop note near the tap, create one safe-water station, and route all mouth-contact uses through that station until authorities lift the notice.
steps: Mark the tap so nobody drinks by habit.
Keep safe water on one clean surface.
Use safe water for drinking, food prep, teeth, and medicine.
Discard ice made before the notice if unsafe.
Write when each safe-water batch was prepared.
warnings: Do not use this routine for chemical do-not-use warnings.
Do not cool safe water in dirty containers.
Do not assume any filter is enough unless rated for the hazard.
common_mistakes: Rinsing baby bottles with unsafe water.
Letting children serve themselves from mixed containers.
alternatives: Run a smaller version of Boil advisory kitchen routine with one responsible person, one helper, and one visible paper note.
Use a safer fallback location or method for Boil advisory kitchen routine if the first choice becomes wet, crowded, contaminated, dark, unstable, or unsafe.
Pause Boil advisory kitchen routine and verify the written record with a second person before moving scarce supplies.
failure_signs: People cannot explain who is responsible for Boil advisory kitchen routine or when the next check happens.
Supplies connected to Boil advisory kitchen routine are unlabelled, damp, contaminated, damaged, missing, or being used for the wrong purpose.
The routine ignores water, hygiene, food contact, and waste boundaries and starts creating new risk instead of reducing existing risk during “Boil advisory kitchen routine”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Boil advisory kitchen routine as a reason to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, fire response, or official local instructions.
Do not use Boil advisory kitchen routine when the location is structurally unsafe, chemically suspicious, smoky, flooded, violent, or otherwise immediately dangerous.
Do not improvise hazardous repairs, dosing, chemical handling, weapon use, or electrical/gas work under the label of Boil advisory kitchen routine.
short_term: For the first hours of Boil advisory kitchen routine, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Boil advisory kitchen routine remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Boil advisory kitchen routine fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
environment_notes: Adapt Boil advisory kitchen routine to the real environment: check water, hygiene, food contact, and waste boundaries, local weather, building condition, water access, light, noise, animals, neighbours, and safe exits before repeating the routine.
